name: Cherry Berry Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Frozen pitted tart cherries, Juice drained from cherries plus 1 tbsp. sugar, divided
1 tbsp Quick tapioca, Plus 1 1/2 tsp cornstarch
recipeInstructions:
1. For the Crust: Combine flour, salt, and baking powder in a bowl. Cut in shortening and butter flavor shortening until just blended into pea size chunks. Add vinegar and water, 1 tbsp at a time, tossing lightly with a fork until dough forms a ball. Divide dough in half. Press between hands to form two 5 to 6-inch pancakes. Roll out one portion of dough into a circle, trimming 1 inch larger than an upside-down 9-inch pie plate. Loosen the dough carefully, fold it into quarters, and press into the pie plate. Trim edge even with the pie plate.
2. For the Cherry Layer: Thaw and drain enough berries to yield 2 to 2 1/2 cups fruit and 1/2 cup juice. Combine cherries, sugar, tapioca, cornstarch, almond extract, and vanilla extract in a large bowl. In a small saucepan, combine cherry juice, sugar, tapioca, and cornstarch. Cook on medium heat, stirring constantly for 3 or 4 minutes until slightly thickened. Cool slightly, then pour over cherries. Mix gently to coat the cherries.
3. For the Cream Cheese Layer: Combine cream cheese, confectioners sugar, almonds, almond extract, and vanilla extract in a bowl. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Spread evenly on the bottom of the unbaked pie shell.
4. For the Raspberry Layer: Combine raspberries, granulated sugar, and cornstarch. Mix carefully. Spoon over the cream cheese layer. If desired, place the mixture in a saucepan and cook briefly until thickened before spooning over the cream cheese layer.
5. Roll the top crust as for the bottom crust and cut into 10 - 1/2-inch strips. Lay 5 strips evenly across the filling. Fold every other strip back. Lay the first strip across in the opposite direction, continuing to fold back every other strip each time you add a cross strip. Trim ends of lattice strips even with the crust. Fold edge under and flute.
6. Brush pastry edges with egg white and sprinkle lightly with granulated sugar. Bake at 400 degrees for 15 minutes. Reduce temperature to 350 degrees and bake an additional 40 minutes, sprinkling with chopped almonds and flaked coconut, if desired, for the last 5 minutes of baking time. Cool until barely warm or room temperature before serving. Makes one 9-inch pie. Note: If using fresh cherries, use 3 to 4 cups in the pie, mashing and pressing additional through a sieve or colander to obtain 1/2 cup juice.
